commit.message:
Fix critical stale closure and race condition bugs in task UI

- useUserPreference: add write version counter to prevent API fetch from
  overwriting setValue calls made during in-flight requests
- ActivityTaskBoard: use refs for preference values to avoid stale closures
  in handleSaveView, handleDeleteView, handleViewModeChange, handleUpdateDefaultView,
  handleResetDefaultView; gate initial override application on loading state
- ActivityFilterBar: fix handleSaveEdit delete-then-save race by reordering;
  fix handleSearch stale filters via ref
- TaskList: fix fetchTasks stale tasks capture via functional updater;
  fix handleLoadMore offset race by passing page to fetchTasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: add nul to gitignore, sync latest changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use selective warning handler and fix contrast filtering for wrapped factors

Two fixes from Codex review of c826679:

1. Replace all.vars() with rownames(attr(tt, "factors")) at 3 call sites
   in predict/model.matrix. all.vars() strips function wrappers, so
   factor(x) in a formula returns "x" but names(contrasts) has "factor(x)".
   The intersect silently dropped the contrast, falling back to defaults.

2. Replace blanket suppressWarnings() with muffle_rank_warnings() helper
   that uses withCallingHandlers to muffle only rank-deficient/singular/
   not-computed diagnostic warnings. Non-diagnostic warnings (CUE
   convergence, collinearity, bandwidth, etc.) now propagate and will
   fail tests if they fire unexpectedly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix reconnect loop: block auto-sync/retry during OAuth popup (#4)

* Stop AI retry loop on Google reauth failure

When runPendingAiRetry hits google_reauth_required, mark that account
in a reauthNeeded Set and skip all subsequent emails for it. This
prevents the retry loop from repeatedly overwriting reauthRequired=true
after the user reconnects.

Also don't advance nextRetryAt for Google auth failures so emails
stay immediately due for retry once the user reconnects Gmail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix reconnect loop: block auto-sync/retry during OAuth popup

The OAuth popup closing fires a window "focus" event which triggered
runAutoSync immediately. At that moment the emails closure was stale
(token:null, reauthRequired:true from before reconnect), causing a
google_reauth_required error that overwrote the reauthRequired:false
set by connectGoogleAccount.

Fix: add connectBusyRef (a mutable ref, always current unlike closure
state) that is set to "google"/"microsoft" before requestGoogleToken
is awaited and cleared in the finally block. runAutoSync checks this
ref at the top and bails out immediately, preventing any retry or
auto-sync from running while an OAuth popup is open.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add VBCS and OPA backup modules + shared_utils refactor

Extends the project with two new backup modules:

vbcsBackup.py — lists VBCS applications via /ic/builder/resources/application/applist,
exports each as a zip archive, and optionally exports Business Object data as
CSV per entity via the VBCS design-time data API.

opaBackup.py — lists Process Automation applications via
/process/api/v1/design/applications, exports each as an .expx archive, and
handles Decision Model applications via the parallel dmnApplications endpoint.

shared_utils.py — new common module providing Vault config loading, OAuth2
client-credentials token acquisition (with per-service prefix and OIC fallback),
OCI Object Storage upload, and ONS notification helpers used by all three modules.

oicMetadataBackup.py refactored to import from shared_utils (removing duplicated
client factories and notification logic).  func.py updated to orchestrate all
three modules based on BACKUP_OIC / BACKUP_VBCS / BACKUP_OPA flags in the
Vault secret.  IAM policy updated to include Object Storage write permission
required for VBCS and OPA uploads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add lobby mode for party setup — fix all critical party bugs

Root cause: connect() auto-sent 'join' during party setup, which placed
players in game rooms prematurely (enemies spawned, ghost players appeared,
wrong room assignments).

Protocol:
- C2S_Join: add lobby?: boolean flag

Server:
- join handler: lobby=true creates playerId without joining a room
  (no welcome sent, no room pollution)
- create_party/join_party: fallback to __lobbyName when playerRoom is null

NetworkManager:
- connect(): add lobbyOnly param, lobby mode skips auto-reconnect
- joinGame(): transition lobby WS to game room (reuses __partyCode
  for correct team assignment via forcedTeam)

Game.ts:
- onPartyCreate/Join: use lobby mode (no room entry during setup)
- onPartyQuick: disconnect lobby + hideParty before character select
- connectMultiplayer: reuse lobby WS via joinGame() when partyRoomCode set

UI.ts:
- applyLang(): add party screen localization (7 elements + placeholder)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Major usability improvements for proof checker

Error visibility:
- Show inline error/incomplete messages directly on proof nodes (was
  previously computed but silently discarded)
- Error nodes show red tooltips, incomplete nodes show blue tooltips
- Summary banner now shows ALL errors (bulleted list when >3), not
  just the first one
- Error inference lines are thicker (2.5px) for visual emphasis

Green flash celebration:
- Full-viewport green flash animation when a proof is validated correct

Angle bracket ligatures:
- < and > now render as ⟨ and ⟩ for closure notation in big-step
- \langle and \rangle LaTeX commands also supported
- Added to ligature hints panel

Robustness:
- All WASM calls wrapped in try-catch with user-friendly error messages
- Check button shows "Checking..." state and prevents double-clicks
- WASM load failures shown as proper error diagnostics
- getNodeAtPath() has bounds checking, all callers null-guarded
- Axiom rule labels now clickable (CSS lock removed)

commit.message:
refactor(brain): remove VPC from Lambdas, drop lambda_sg

Lambdas only use AWS APIs (Data API, Bedrock, EventBridge, Secrets
Manager) so VPC is unnecessary. This eliminates slow ENI cleanup on
delete (was 20 min), reduces cold starts, and simplifies networking.
ECS tasks remain in VPC for EFS access.

fix: resolve dashboard stats and hover state bugs (PX-835, PX-864)

PX-835: Dashboard now fetches real stats from database
- Added getDashboardStats() to query forms, clients, calls, submissions
- Created /api/dashboard/stats API endpoint

Also fixed pre-existing type errors:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: eliminate redundant auth callbacks to speed up login/logout

AuthContext:
- Remove fetchAndSetUserProfile from the SIGNED_IN subscriber.
  login() already calls it eagerly before navigate(), so the subscriber
  was making a second identical profiles query on every login.
  getSession() in initializeAuth handles the page-reload case.

AppContext:
- Gate fetchInitialData() behind SIGNED_IN and INITIAL_SESSION events
  only. Previously it fired on TOKEN_REFRESHED too, silently reloading
  all 13 tables every ~1 hour in the background and on every auth event.

Net effect: login goes from 2 sequential profile round-trips to 1,
and background token refreshes no longer trigger a full data reload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: enforce AGENTS.md §11 performance constraints in cache integration (M9, #179) (#249)

Add scope isolation to InvocationCache compilation keys so that
--project-scoped invocations cannot serve compilations from unrelated
solution projects. Add single-workspace guard to RoslynWorkspaceService
via Interlocked.CompareExchange. Verify MSBuild single-pass registration
is unaffected by cache paths.

Changes:
- InvocationCache.SetScope() prefixes compilation keys with entry-point
  path (first-write-wins via Interlocked.CompareExchange)
- ApplicationRunner.RunAsync binds cache scope after input resolution
- RoslynWorkspaceService.LoadAsync guards against repeated workspace
  creation
- 4 new InvocationCacheTests: scope isolation, cache hit, first-write
  semantics, template independence

docs: update README for single-runtime architecture

- Architecture diagram: single Tauri process with embedded daemon
- Quick Start: removed "start the daemon" step
- Build from Source: simplified to make install / make dev / make build
- Tech Stack: IPC updated from HTTP/WebSocket to Tauri commands/events
- Configuration: removed daemon_port, added LABALABA_DATA_DIR note

repository.name: docker4python
repository.description: Docker-based Python web stack (works for Django)

commit.message:
feat: 按 DUPLICATE KEY 要求对列排序

KEY 列按主键声明顺序置于列定义最前，非 KEY 列保持原始 SQL 顺序。
新增 KEY 列缺失警告：当 PRIMARY KEY 中的列名在列定义中找不到时
（如大小写不一致、列名拼写错误），输出 ⚠️ 注释提示人工核查。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
